advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Nutritional requirements per kilogram of body weight are significantly higher in children than in adults due to rapid growth and development. Iron, calcium, vitamin D, and omega-3 DHA are the most critical nutrients for cognitive and skeletal development in children. Establishing healthy eating behaviors and food preferences in early childhood has measurable lifelong dietary impact.
research_findings: Exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months provides optimal nutrition and immune protection in infants per WHO guidelines. Iron deficiency in children under 5 is associated with measurable cognitive and motor development delays. Excess sugar intake in childhood accelerates dental caries and establishes neural reward pathways associated with addictive eating patterns.
current_focus_areas: Building age-stratified nutritional guidelines for infants, toddlers, school-age children, and adolescents; developing parent-facing dietary guidance tools; researching the role of early gut microbiome development in long-term health outcomes.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Lifecycle & Specialized Population Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 21 (Food Texture Analysis). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 39%.
